title: YQ-400 Monitor droid patrol ship

A product of the jointly funded design team of the [[Loronar Corporation/Legends|Loronar Corporation]] and [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] known as [[The Nerfworks]], the '''YQ-400 Monitor Droid Patrol Ship''' was a small (36 meters long) ship designed to work in areas where high docking fees and trade tariffs provided most of a system's profits. Its primary job was to detect, locate and follow ships entering the system, although the lack of [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] limited the ship to local areas only.

==Characteristics==
The YQ-400 monitor droid patrol ship was model of [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] in the [[YQ series]] of droid patrol ships. The vessels were built with light patrol of peaceful [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] in mind, given that the ships were not heavily armed enough to take on most [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] or [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] vessels in [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]]. The vessels were also not smart enough to be of any military use. As a patrol ship, the YQ-400's programming would assign it a predefined vector within the assigned system, and the droid would patrol it. Upon encountering any other ship in the vector, a YQ-400 would report the encounter and hail the other vessel, then following and periodically reporting on the ship until given an all clear by the local [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] control. The droids would only open fire upon other vessels if attacked first, although the YQ-400s were very difficult to locate due to the fact they only made use of passive [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]]. While this programming meant attackers always had an advantage over the patrol ships, it meant that there was no risk of them attacking friendly vessels.

Each YQ-400 cost 175,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] new and was armed with two sets of dual [[Ion cannon/Legends|heavy ion cannons]] and two [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]. They required zero crew, but carried twenty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] worth of consumables despite having no cargo capacity. At thirty-six [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length the ships were relatively small, and were designed with a long cylindrical shape with an [[Engine/Legends|engine]] at the back, although the droids lacked a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]].

==History==
The YQ-400 model was one of just over a dozen models of droid starships designed and produced at [[The Nerfworks]], a shipyard lab funded jointly by the starship manufacturing [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] and the droid design company [[Loronar Corporation/Legends|Loronar Corporation]]. They, along with the rest of the YQ series, were used most commonly in systems with heavy shipping and commerce, where stationing manned vessels in every approach vector was financially unfeasible but where ship tariffs and docking fees were lucrative enough to make the droid ships worth employing. The YQ-400 was the most popular of the YQ series, and a used YQ-400 was worth a reduced 100,000 credits.

==Behind the scenes==
The YQ-400 was first introduced in ''[[Droid Starships]]'' an article written by [[Owen K.C. Stephens]] and published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 10|tenth issue]] of ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' in [[2002]]. The article provided stats for the ships use in [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]].
